intent.operator_problem: Pit bosses cannot formally end a patron visit during the gaming day. Visits persist until rollover or exclusion auto-close, inflating active-player counts, leaving an open-ended compliance record, and blocking coherent session continuation. Separately, the already-built Start From Previous workflow dead-ends in production because closed slip clicks open a read-only slip viewer instead of the continuation modal. The Closed Sessions panel also mislabels closed rating slips as sessions, confusing slip-level and visit-level lifecycle objects.
intent.pilot_fit: This feature belongs in the current slice because exclusion enforcement and session-gated hardening made the visit lifecycle gap visible. The underlying hooks, APIs, RPCs, and modal components largely exist, but End Visit is not purely wiring; it is a compound operator workflow requiring explicit ordering, failure handling, and compliance-safe outcomes. Start From Previous wiring repair and terminology cleanup are included as adjacent pilot repairs so the resulting closed state is not incoherent.
success_outcomes: Pit boss can end a patron visit from the rating slip modal.
Ending a visit finalizes all open or paused slips through rpc_close_rating_slip rather than raw status mutation.
Visit is not marked closed if any required slip finalization fails.
No orphaned open slips remain after operator-initiated visit close.
Closed Slips panel is labeled with slip or segment terminology rather than session terminology.
Clicking a closed rated slip opens StartFromPreviousModal instead of the read-only rating slip viewer.
Start From Previous creates a new visit and a new rating slip; it never reopens a closed slip or visit.
Continuation eligibility rules are explicit rather than inferred.
explicit_exclusions: Visit-level grouping in the Closed Slips panel
Dedicated table or seat picker for Start From Previous
Changes to gaming day rollover behavior
Loyalty accrual logic changes beyond invoking existing close behavior
Additional role-based authorization for End Visit in this pilot slice

adjacent_rejected.idea: Add End Visit to the seat right-click context menu
adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Natural ergonomic fit and previously identified as a candidate surface
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Production table layout does not currently render SeatContextMenu; wiring it would expand scope beyond the pilot slice

adjacent_rejected.idea: Close only the visit and leave slips as-is
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Would leave orphaned open slips and break compliance-safe close behavior

adjacent_rejected.idea: Use raw UPDATE to close slips instead of RPC calls
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Bypasses theo computation, pause handling, constraints, and audit logging

adjacent_rejected.idea: Build a dedicated table or seat picker for Start From Previous
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Existing seat-click flow is sufficient for MVP and a new picker would introduce unnecessary UI and state complexity

adjacent_rejected.idea: Show visits instead of slips in the Closed Slips panel
adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Fixes semantic mismatch at the data layer
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Requires new API or RPC work; relabeling is the pilot-bounded correction

adjacent_rejected.idea: Reopen a closed slip instead of creating a new one
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Violates temporal integrity and sealed audit history

entities.name: visit
entities.description: Patron floor-presence session for a gaming day; source of record for visit lifecycle

entities.name: rating_slip
entities.description: Table-segment telemetry record within a visit; closed through RPC-bound compliance logic

entities.name: closed_slip_row
entities.description: Dashboard-facing row representing a closed rating slip shown in the Closed Slips panel

entities.name: recent_session_summary
entities.description: Visit-level aggregate returned for Start From Previous selection

entities.name: policy_snapshot
entities.description: Immutable close and accrual policy snapshot captured on slip creation and used for compliant close calculation

entities.name: pending_continuation_context
entities.description: Transient UI state storing the selected closed visit and awaiting empty-seat placement

capabilities.name: end_visit_from_rating_slip_modal
capabilities.description: Operator initiates visit closure from the active rating slip modal

capabilities.name: finalize_open_slips_for_visit
capabilities.description: System finalizes all open or paused slips for a visit through rpc_close_rating_slip

capabilities.name: close_visit_record
capabilities.description: System marks the visit ended only after required slip finalization succeeds

capabilities.name: relabel_closed_slips_surface
capabilities.description: System copy uses slip or segment terminology rather than session terminology for closed slip rows

capabilities.name: open_start_from_previous_modal
capabilities.description: Closed rated slip click opens the continuation modal instead of the read-only slip viewer

capabilities.name: continue_visit_from_history
capabilities.description: System creates a new visit and a new rating slip from a selected historical closed visit

capabilities.name: enforce_continuation_eligibility
capabilities.description: System applies explicit rules determining which recent visits may be resumed

outbound.name: audit log write per closed slip

permissions: place continued player into empty seat
restrictions: Pilot accepts current modal-level authorization posture; no additional End Visit role gate is added in this slice

permissions: same operational actions as pit_boss where current UI already allows them
restrictions: No new special override path is introduced by this feature

trigger_events.name: patron_departs_floor
preconditions: operator can open the active rating slip modal

trigger_events.name: operator_confirms_end_visit
preconditions: active or paused slip(s) exist for the visit
required close preconditions are satisfied or explicitly handled

trigger_events.name: returning_patron_requires_continuation
preconditions: closed slip row exists for player context entry
eligible recent closed visit exists or can be determined

timing_constraints: End Visit occurs mid-shift during active gaming day operations
Visit must not be marked closed before required slip finalization succeeds
Continuation placement occurs after selection of a closed visit and before creation of the new slip

business_rules.statement: Operator-initiated visit closure must finalize all open or paused slips through rpc_close_rating_slip rather than raw status mutation.
Visit must not be marked closed if any required slip-finalization step fails.
Closed slip rows are context entry points only; they are not the objects being reopened or resumed.
Start From Previous creates a new visit and a new rating slip and never reopens a closed visit or slip.
User-facing copy must use slip or segment language for closed slip rows and visit or session language for continuation candidates.
Continuation eligibility rules must be explicit rather than inferred from whichever recent session happens to appear.
Pilot does not add new UI surfaces or redesign authorization posture beyond the existing modal-enabled workflow.

invariants: No orphaned open slips remain after successful operator-initiated visit close.
Closed slips remain immutable and are not reopened.
Theo for operator-closed slips is computed from policy_snapshot rather than forced to zero.
Compliance chain remains intact across operator close and continuation.
Scope expansion requires amendment rather than downstream invention.
decision_notes: Feature spine is operator-attested visit closure for the current lifecycle posture.
Closed Slips terminology cleanup, Start From Previous wiring, and continuation eligibility rules are adjacent repairs necessary to avoid incoherent closed-state UX.
Bulk close RPC is not required for MVP if failure semantics are explicitly defined and safe.

loop.action: identifies departing patron and opens active rating slip modal from occupied seat
closes all open or paused slips for the visit via rpc_close_rating_slip
loop.system_response: finalizes slip-level theo, pause state, and audit trail

loop.action: marks visit ended through existing close endpoint
loop.system_response: visit is closed only if required slip finalization succeeds
seat frees up, closed slips appear, toast communicates outcome

loop.action: opens Closed Slips panel and identifies a closed rated slip
loop.system_response: slip row is available as player/context entry point
system resolves player context instead of opening read-only slip viewer

loop.action: fetches recent visit-level sessions for the player
loop.system_response: StartFromPreviousModal opens with eligible historical visits

loop.action: selects the closed visit to continue
prompts operator to select an empty seat
clicks empty seat on destination table
loop.system_response: system validates continuation context and destination
creates new visit and new rating slip if gates pass
patron is back in play with preserved history chain

loop.action: updates closed-surface copy to slip-consistent terminology
loop.system_response: panel and continuation language remain semantically consistent

required_existing: GAP-EXCL-ENFORCE-001 Layer 1 exclusion checks in rpc_start_rating_slip
occupied-seat click opening the rating slip modal
missing_dependencies: Potential schema/API addition if recent session eligibility requires closure reason distinctions not currently present in RecentSessionsDTO
assumptions: Every slip eligible for operator-initiated close contains or can supply the minimum rating inputs required by rpc_close_rating_slip to compute compliant theo from policy_snapshot.
Sequential rpc_close_rating_slip orchestration is acceptable for MVP only if safe failure semantics are explicitly defined and partial compliance state is prevented or recoverably surfaced.
Recent-session data can be made sufficient to distinguish continuation-eligible visits from merely historical closed visits.
Pilot can rely on existing UI patterns rather than introducing bespoke components for missing-rating or pending-continuation handling.

outcomes.statement: Operator can end a patron visit from the rating slip modal.
Operator-initiated close uses compliant slip-finalization behavior.
Visit close cannot succeed with incomplete required slip finalization.
No orphaned open slips remain after successful End Visit.
Closed-surface terminology reflects slips rather than sessions.
Closed rated slip click opens StartFromPreviousModal.
Continuation creates a new visit and a new rating slip.
Continuation eligibility is explicit and compliance-safe.

open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold: Whether minimum rating inputs, including average_bet where applicable, must be present before End Visit may finalize a slip
Whether the pending-continuation toast should expose cancel behavior or timeout under existing UI patterns
Whether continuation eligibility can remain within current RecentSessionsDTO shape or requires a schema addition for closure reason or similar state
